title: GrabDesk
description: GrabDesk is my real-time commerce platform focused on live admin clarity, recommendation relevance, and secure checkout logic.

content.text: GrabDesk started when I saw how much admin time gets wasted in stale dashboards and delayed inventory updates.
I needed admins to operate from one live truth, and I needed users to get recommendations that felt relevant even without massive data volume.
Socket.IO handled real-time dashboard sync, while tag-based recommendation scoring gave contextual suggestions with lightweight behavior signals.
Real-time updates were not visual polish. They were operational safety.
I enforced server-side price recalculation at checkout so the backend never trusted client-submitted totals. I also kept images in Cloudinary to avoid bloating MongoDB with binary payloads.
In commerce, silent trust in the client is how expensive bugs are born.
For stock consistency under concurrent operations, I switched to atomic update patterns so inventory state stayed correct under load.
GrabDesk taught me to balance three priorities at once: fast operations, secure transactions, and recommendations that still feel personal.

content.suggestion: Admin product update instantly broadcasting to all connected dashboards
